jobs.title: Fall or Winter Internship at State Farm Insurance - Adam Jurs
jobs.description: Employer: State Farm Insurance - Adam Jurs Expires: 06/23/2026 We are one of the leading offices out of 20,000 independent Agents Nation-wide in the areas of B2B, Retirement Planning, and multiple other product lines. We are looking to teach specific skills such as: Marketing, Advertising, Leadership Development, Human Resources, Insurance, Sales, General Entrepreneurial behaviors, Event Planning and Management, Digital marketing, etc...If you are looking for part-time or full-time work, we can be flexible during any season, but this is an in-office position and can lead into work from home/school after training. jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Sales and Digital Marketing Intern- BLK Mama at Vower
jobs.description: Employer: Vower Expires: 06/24/2026 Sales and Digital Marketing Intern Monthly Stipend: $350- $500/month (Looking to hire after internship) Company OverviewBLKMAMA Company is a hospitality and wellness brand rooted in the mission of cultivating Generational Health. We guide individuals on the journey of unlearning old patterns and relearning healthier, more mindful practices to achieve peak mental, physical, and emotional wellness. Our tools and inspiration empower personal transformation while building a legacy of thriving for future generations—from nourishing the body and nurturing self-love to strengthening relationships and stepping into your best self every day. Job TitleSales and Digital Marketing Intern Location Remote (Global) Start Date June 2026 Duration 3-6 months Hours 15 hours per week Role Overview BLKMAMA Company is seeking a strong-willed, confident Sales and Digital Marketing Intern to join our remote team. This role focuses on content creation and social media management across Instagram, Facebook, YouTube, and TikTok, with opportunities to support sales, customer engagement, and brand growth in the wellness space. The owner has a clear vision and foundational know-how; the ideal candidate is a self-starter who brings creative guidance, great energy, and proactive initiative to execute and elevate that vision. With company expansion, this internship offers strong potential to evolve into a full-time role.Key ResponsibilitiesSocial Media Management & Content Creation: Create and post engaging content (posts, stories, reels, videos) on Instagram, Facebook, YouTube, and TikTok to boost brand awareness and community interaction. Develop a consistent brand voice and aesthetic that resonates with our audience, highlighting Generational Health, wellness transformation, and mindful living. Support content for marketing campaigns, including photos, videos, and graphics. Sales Support: Assist in identifying and reaching out to potential customers or partners, particularly through professional and social platforms. Support sales initiatives by creating promotional materials and tracking leads. Customer Experience: Engage with followers across social media, responding to inquiries and comments professionally and promptly. Foster community by interacting with our audience and promoting a positive, empowering brand image centered on healing and legacy. Administrative Tasks:Maintain organized records of social media metrics, sales leads, and campaign performance. Assist with scheduling posts, coordinating campaigns, and other ad-hoc duties. Required Skills & Qualifications Active presence and proficiency in Instagram, Facebook, YouTube, and TikTok for content creation and audience engagement. Experience creating engaging short-form video content, such as reels or TikTok videos. Strong organizational skills for managing social media schedules and sales leads. Excellent written and verbal communication skills for customer interactions and content creation. Strong-willed, confident self-starter with great energy, creative guidance, and a proactive attitude to drive vision execution. Passion for wellness, hospitality, and cultivating Generational Health. Comfortable working independently in a remote, fast-paced global environment. Preferred Qualifications Experience with Editing & Digital Content tools (e.g., Photoshop, Illustrator) for graphic design or video editing is a plus. Familiarity with wellness brands or mindfulness-focused initiatives. Compensation & Benefits This internship offers a $350–$500 monthly stipend. Interns will gain hands-on experience in digital marketing, sales, and customer engagement within the wellness industry, boosting their resume and professional network. With company growth, this role has strong potential to transition into a full-time position. Intern will also receive complimentary 12-month access to SAP training access and certifications courtesy of Vower, providing a competitive edge in the job market. What We’re Looking For We’re seeking an intern who is a confident self-starter, aligns with BLKMAMA’s vision of Generational Health, and brings the energy and creativity to make it happen. We value bold individuals who take initiative, offer fresh ideas, and are passionate about building a brand that transforms lives and legacies. If you’re excited to contribute to a mission-driven wellness company with room to grow into a full-time role, this is your opportunity!BLKMAMA Company is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and interns.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Brand Partnership Strategist at Memorize Trunks
jobs.description: Employer: Memorize Trunks Expires: 06/25/2026 Brand Partnership Strategist (Project-Based, Summer 2026)Memorize Trunks | Remote | Paid StipendAre you a marketing student who wants real work to talk about in your first full-time interview? This is it.Memorize Trunks is a luxury keepsake trunk brand — custom, design-forward, and growing. This summer, we're hiring one motivated college student to build our brand partnership program from scratch. You'll own the strategy, the outreach, and the results.What you'll actually do:Research and develop a collaboration strategy targeting boutique brands across weddings, gifting, new baby, lifestyle, and eventsPitch and negotiate partnerships with real founders and brand ownersExecute the strategy and document the outcomesBy the end, you'll have a written strategy, completed outreach, and live partnership results — the kind of work you can walk into any interview and own.You're a great fit if:You're a rising junior or senior studying marketing, communications, or PR with some hands-on marketing experience — internships, campus organizations, freelance work, or your own projects countYou genuinely love luxury and lifestyle brands — you follow them, you notice them, you understand themYou can write a sharp, confident cold pitch and aren't afraid to send itYou work well independently and take initiative without being managedThe details:Paid stipend~40 hours over 4 to 6 weeksFully remote, flexible scheduleIndependent contractorTo apply (please read carefully):Email gayle@memorizeyourlife.com with the subject line Summer Partnership Project. Keep it short — two or three paragraphs max. Tell us why you want this, and name one brand you think Memorize Trunks should partner with and why. Of course, please include your resume.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Festival App Design & Product Coordinator at The Big Dill®
jobs.description: Employer: The Big Dill® Expires: 06/25/2026 The Big Dill® is hiring a paid, project-based Festival App Design & Product Coordinator to help build The Big Dill Pickle Pass, the official app experience for our 2026 three-city tour across Baltimore, Arlington/DFW, and Philadelphia.This is not a coding role. The Pickle Pass will be built inside an existing event app platform such as Grandstand. The role is closer to product development: helping structure the app, organize features, build content, test mobile flows, improve the guest experience, and make sure the app is useful during a real festival.The Pickle Pass will help guests choose their city, view maps, browse vendors, follow schedules, receive push notifications, complete scavenger hunts, enter photo challenges, vote for Best in Brine, submit costume contest entries, unlock prizes, and access FAQ / Ask AI support.Strong skills in Canva, Figma, Photoshop, Google Sheets, Monday.com, mobile app tools, QR flows, short guest-facing copy, and organized product thinking are required.Responsibilities include creating app content, organizing vendor and sponsor listings, building feature trackers, helping design badges and QR signage, drafting push notifications, mapping scavenger hunt and photo challenge flows, testing the app on mobile devices, and improving anything that feels confusing.Before applying: research The Big Dill and Grandstand. In your cover letter, include:What would you personally want to see in a festival app that would make you actually use it during an event?One Grandstand feature you think could be useful for The Big Dill Pickle Pass.One idea to make the app feel fun, not like homework.One example of design, app, content, product, or project work you have created.Generic applications will not be considered.Ideal candidates understand festivals, food events, pop-ups, concerts, social media, and what makes people scan, click, play, and participate at live events.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Events Assistant at The O Agency
jobs.description: Employer: The O Agency Expires: 06/25/2026 About the RoleWe're hiring a part-time Events Assistant to support both the planning side and the sales/booking side of our business. You'll be the connective tissue between our clients, our production team, and our calendar — making sure inquiries get answered fast, events get planned tight, and nothing falls through the cracks.This is a hybrid role: you'll handle most planning, client communication, and booking work remotely, then show up on-site for key events to assist our lead coordinator and AV crew. It's ideal for someone who has 2–4 years of events, hospitality, or AV-adjacent experience and wants flexible part-time hours with real responsibility.What You'll DoRespond to incoming event inquiries via email, phone, and our website within 24 hours.Qualify leads: gather event date, venue, scope, budget, and AV needs before passing to the owner.Build and send proposals, quotes, and contracts using our templates.Maintain the booking calendar; prevent double-bookings and flag scheduling conflicts.Coordinate event logistics: timelines, run-of-show, venue contacts, vendor coordination, and crew call sheets.Liaise with clients in the lead-up to events — gather final details, confirm load-in times, and manage last-minute changes.Attend select events on-site to assist with client check-in, vendor coordination, and supporting the AV lead.Follow up post-event: thank-you notes, feedback collection, invoice tracking, and nudging repeat-booking opportunities.Keep our CRM, Google Drive, and shared calendars clean, current, and organized.Help with light sales support: outreach to past clients, referral follow-ups, and basic pipeline tracking.What We're Looking For2–4 years of experience in event coordination, hospitality, venue management, AV production, or a sales/booking role at an event-services company.Comfortable on the phone and over email with clients — polished, warm, and responsive.Highly organized; you actually enjoy spreadsheets, calendars, and checklists.Reliable transportation and a valid driver's license (you'll travel to venues across metro Atlanta).Available some evenings and weekends — events don't all happen 9–5.Basic comfort with Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Calendar, Gmail).Calm under pressure: when a mic goes out 10 minutes before doors, you don't panic.Nice to HaveWorking knowledge of AV terminology (lavs vs. handhelds, line array, DMX, gain staging, etc.).Experience with a CRM (HoneyBook, Dubsado, HubSpot, 17hats, Goldcast, etc.).Existing relationships with Atlanta venues, planners, or production vendors.Experience writing proposals or invoicing clients.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Communications Intern at Leadership Impact Strategies
jobs.description: Employer: Leadership Impact Strategies Expires: 06/25/2026 Jacksonville, FL (Hybrid) | Part-Time | $20/hr | 10 Weeks | June 16 – August 24, 2026 We are reviewing applications on a rolling basis and encourage early submission. This position begins June 16, 2026.About the RoleLeadership Impact Strategies (LIS) is a Jacksonville-based executive consulting firm that helps mid-market organizations rapidly improve team alignment, accountability, and execution. Our clients are C-suite and senior leaders across financial services, insurance, commercial real estate, and professional services.This is a high-ownership role with direct access to the CEO. This summer, we're bringing on a graduate-level Communications Intern to help build the CEO's visibility and thought leadership presence. Your work will reach real audiences, not sit in a classroom case study. What You'll Own1. Podcast and Speaking Outreach Research relevant industry podcasts and speaking opportunities, draft pitch materials, and maintain a tracking system for submissions and responses. The goal is to consistently get the CEO in front of the right audiences.2. Earned Visibility Research Identify and track local and regional recognition opportunities, including business journal lists, industry nominations, and community recognitions such as the Jacksonville Business Journal's annual awards. You'll assess fit, flag the strongest opportunities, and lead the drafting of submissions.3. Thought Leadership Drafting Ghostwrite articles, LinkedIn content, and short-form pieces in the CEO's voice. This requires strong writing instincts and the ability to translate executive ideas into polished, audience-ready content.4. Speaking Engagement Amplification Repurpose completed speaking engagements into written assets, including recap articles, LinkedIn posts, and media pitch angles, to extend the reach of content that has already been delivered. Who We're Looking ForEnrolled in or recently completed a graduate program in Strategic Communications, Public Relations, or JournalismExceptionally strong writer with a clear, professional, and concise styleSelf-directed, proactive in providing updates, and comfortable working without close supervisionSkilled at writing in someone else's voice with minimal back-and-forthFamiliar with LinkedIn as a professional visibility platformExperience with media databases and podcast directories is a plus The DetailsPay: $20/hourHours: 8 hours per week, flexibleDuration: 10 weeks, June 16 through August 24, 2026Location: Hybrid (Jacksonville, FL area preferred, though fully remote will be considered for strong candidates)Looking ahead: Strong performers will be considered for ongoing part-time work How to ApplyPlease send to: concierge@leadershipimpactstrategies.com:Your resumeOne original writing sample that demonstrates your ability to write in an executive or professional voice. No AI-generated content.A sample pitch email you would send to a podcast host or event organizer to secure a guest speaking appearance. This can be original or from previous work.A 90-second video (optional) answering this question: What executive or brand do you think does thought leadership well, and why? We want to hear how you think, not just how you write.Finalists will be asked to complete a brief skills assessment prior to interviews.We read every application and will respond to every finalist.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Manchester, NH-based. Fall 2026 Social Media/Marketing Internship at All Nutrient
jobs.description: Employer: All Nutrient Expires: 06/25/2026 ResponsibilitiesHelp manage social media accounts including Facebook, Twitter, Google+, Instagram, etc. This incorporates:Posting daily content.Monitoring, listening and responding to users in a quick and effective manner.Asking good, relevant, and thought-provoking questions in an engaging way.Setting the tone for the community, being present, and enforcing community guidelines.Work with other creatives to manage the content and design of all the company’s social media sites in order to heighten engagement, increase SEO, and promote the company.Facilitate the creation of new marketing campaigns and brand promotions for the company via social media.Coordinate with other creatives in the company to coordinate campaigns and brand promotions to fit in to the overall marketing and promotional goals of the company.Keep a pulse on the industry and maintain frequent presence on relevant beauty sites and blogs to increase the company’s exposure in the industry.Monitor trends in social media tools, applications, channels, design and strategy.Brainstorm and develop processes for capturing customer online reviews and monitor these online ratings accordingly.Research and write relevant content for the company’s social media sites, including blogs, landing pages, email follow ups, etc.Suggest and plan events to promote brand awareness and/or educate our current customers.Research and focus on potential buyers and their personal needs in order to engage them with content and information.Adapt content specifically for each social network to yield more clicks and leads.Dig into data, analyzing data, and drawing actionable insights. This includes macro data (like overall reach, leads generated, leads nurtured, customer cases supported) all the way down to micro data (like individual experiments around content positioning).Direct followers' questions or comments appropriately and provide a course of action for resolution.Work with the marketing team to gain creative understanding of product positioning and packaging/brand vision.Establish project direction and help ensure deadlines are met. Other ResponsibilitiesAssist in the creation and marketing of education events.Support our customer service team if needed. Qualifications:Bachelor’s Degree (or pursing)Excellent writing and language skillsIn-depth knowledge and understanding of social media platformsStrong organization skills, communication skills and decision-making skillsAbility to troubleshoot and solve problemsOutstanding customer service skills and conflict resolution principlesUnderstanding of the company and its products.Proficient knowledge of Adobe suite.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Fashion Class Instructor at Future Fashion Stars
jobs.description: Employer: Future Fashion Stars Expires: 06/25/2026 Fall 2026 Fashion Internship - Fashion Class Instructor Are you a fashion major who wants real, hands on experience working on creating a collection while building leadership skills that employers value? Join Future Fashion Stars this 2026 - 2027 school year and become of part of our passionate enrichment education team delivering Concept, Illustration/Sketching, Design, Sewing Basics, the Importance of Sustainability in Fashion & more to elementary and middle school students. This is more than an instructor role - it's an internship for a thriving start up Future Fashion Stars where you will design, create, build and teach kids about fashion while developing processional communication and leadership skills. You will also have the opportunity to work side by side the founder in enhancing the enrichment experience thru evolving educational materials and developing new classroom capsules. Future Fashion Stars launched in summer 2023 with one summer camp in one location. In just three years, we have over 50 locations in Ohio and New York. We are growing fast. Work with industry professionals and university graduates. Fun engaging curriculum. Activity Based. Position Details. Locations (schools and recreation centers across the area)Cleveland Ohio Columbus OhioCincinnati OhioSchedule: Saturdays 930 - 1130am & 1 - 3pm and Weekdays After School 330 - 530pm Compensation: Paid $20 per hour, must commit to the 4 - 6 week class series Transportation: you must have a car to reach various camp locations across Cleveland. Why This Role? Real World Fashion Experience: Create a fashion collection with the students, you will actively apply your fashion knowledge. Develop Teaching and Leadership Skills: Guide kids ages 6 - 12 thru creative capsules and design challenges. Collaborate on Special Projects: Contribute to curriculum enhancements, innovate class capsules, bring your best ideas. Expand your Fashion Knowledge: Work with industry donated materials, support students in bringing their creative upcycle ideas to life, lead the production for the family fashion show at the end of camp. Key Responsibilities: Preparing materials and supporting Camp Creative Director in managing curriculum, camp flow. Work side by side founder to create new materials and class capsules. Required Skills and Qualifications:Passionate about teaching the next generation about sustainability in fashion. Available to work in the camps on location Strong interpersonal skills. Sewing (a plus) Previous experience working with kids (a plus) How to apply: Text 424.280.0254 with interest. Email your resume & letter of interest to Krista@FutureFashionStars.Com Join us this 2026 - 2027 school year!! Earn $20 an hour for an incredible experience shaping the minds of Future Fashion Stars!

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Marketing Content Intern at BeFreed
jobs.description: Employer: BeFreed Expires: 06/25/2026 Marketing Content Intern 📍 San Francisco, CA · Part-time or Full-time · In-person / HybridAbout UsWe're an seed round AI startup focusing on education consumer products. Over the course of 7 months, we acquired 500K users + tens of millions of views. And we are now looking for someone to SCALE the growth trajectories together. If you are familiar with Cluely, Cal AI, Tolan, Ditto, Gleam, and you want to become a part of rising trend. Join us. The RoleWe're looking for creators or growth interns — someone who lives and breathes short-form content, knows what makes a video stop a scroll, and gets genuinely excited about helping ideas reach millions of people.If you've coached creators, run your own UGC brand, or have opinions about why some TikToks blow up and others don't, we want to meet you.What You'll DoConcept, film, and script short-form UGC / short-form videos for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and beyondBe on camera — you'll be a face of the brand (or help us find the people who should be)Strategize the direction of growth and work with millions of views content creators. Develop and test creative hooks, formats, and storytelling angles to drive views and installsIterate fast: analyze what's working, kill what isn't, double down on winnersContribute ideas to help us build toward our first 10M, then 100M views collectivelyWho You AreYou have real experience in content creation, filmmaking, UGC, or scriptwriting — or just are creative and fun with tons of great ideas we can explore together to make everything VIRAL. You love self-expression! You love to work with creators!You know what it takes to shoot a good video + you know how to make something go viral. You love to contribute to an environment where you can shape the growth of an early stage product and can co-create the product togetherYou want to establish a personal brand and we help you and us to get there. Bonus: you've done viral videos/short films/long films before + you are passionate about building/you are familiar with products like Cluely, Cal AI, Tolan, Ditto, Gleam, Brainrot etc. What We OfferA front-row seat to building a consumer AI company from 10 to 1000000+Real ownership — your content will actually ship and reach real audiencesHelp write the playbook on how AI-education brands grow through creator contentCompensation better than our competitors A team that values speed, creativity, and your individual voice

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Short-Form Video Editor at The Big Dill®
jobs.description: Employer: The Big Dill® Expires: 06/26/2026 Short-Form Social Video Editor (Please read the full description)The Big Dill® is hiring a paid, project-based Short-Form Social Video Editor to help turn our existing event footage into weekly TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts content.This is a perfect summer internship for a creative editor who wants real experience turning trends into content for a national festival brand with a 1.3M+ social following.We will supply the footage, brand assets, past event clips, food content, crowd moments, Dilly mascot clips, vendor footage, and access to copyright-safe music resources such as Motion Array. Bonus if you are skilled enough to develop original content concepts from scratch, including scripts, voiceovers, simple graphics, memes, or edits that do not rely entirely on existing footage.Experience with AI editing tools is a major plus. We are actively exploring AI-assisted video workflows, including ways to bring Dilly, The Big Dill mascot, to life through short-form content, motion, character clips, social edits, and branded video concepts.Your job is to understand what is trending on social, translate those trends into The Big Dill’s brand voice, and create fast, clickable short-form videos that drive attention, engagement, and traffic.This role is about editing and creative packaging, not filming. The right person knows how to take existing footage and make it feel timely, native to social, and worth clicking.We are looking for someone with 5+ years of video editing experience, strong short-form instincts, and a portfolio of edited work. Applications without a portfolio, reel, TikTok/Instagram account, YouTube channel, or work samples will not be considered.Each week, you will pitch 3 to 5 short-form content ideas and produce 2 to 3 quick-turn edits using existing Big Dill content. Most videos will be short, punchy, and built around hooks, text overlays, captions, music, meme timing, food shots, crowd energy, and clear calls to action.Voiceover ability is a major plus. Marketing instincts are important. We want someone who understands how content supports ticket sales, brand growth, sponsor value, and fan engagement.Strong skills in CapCut, Premiere Pro, Final Cut, DaVinci Resolve, AI editing tools, or similar editing platforms are required.Before applying: Please review The Big Dill on Instagram and TikTok at @bigdillpickleparty. In your cover letter, include:A link to your portfolio, reel, or edited workOne current TikTok/Reels trend you think could work for The Big DillOne Big Dill content angle you think could drive clicks or engagementYour preferred editing softwareWhether you are comfortable doing voiceoversAny AI video/editing tools you have experience withYour weekly availabilityGeneric applications will not be considered.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Summer Marketing Intern at Thrive Hair Bar
jobs.description: Employer: Thrive Hair Bar Expires: 06/26/2026 Thrive Hair Bar is looking for its Summer marketing intern!Each season we have the opportunity to work with creative students who have great ideas for how to best market services, products, and experiences. These students have great attention to detail, learn & grow from their experiences, and thrive on constructive feedback. Is Thrive Hair Bar's next great marketing intern you?We're looking for someone who is passionate about disrupting the American beauty standard, who is passionate about helping people feel comfortable, confident, and empowered in their true selves, and who will be comfortable with constantly adapting to a rapidly evolving work environment by helping Thrive Hair Bar realize it's marketing strategy that includes live, in-person events, social media (Instagram & Facebook), virtual events, classes, and frequent collaborations with industry leaders and medical professionals.About Thrive Hair BarThrive Hair Bar DC is a premier, luxury natural hair salon proudly located in one of Washington, DC’s most vibrant and historic neighborhoods, H Street NE. We specialize in natural hair styling services, including twists, locs, braids, and blowouts, tailored to enhance and celebrate the beauty of kinky, coily, and curly hair. In addition to our expert styling, we offer personalized consultations and a curated selection of retail products to empower our clients to embrace and care for their unique hair journey. Committed to fostering a sense of community and empowerment, we also host local events that celebrate diversity and promote the normalization of natural hair in every facet of life, from professional to personal to luxurious. At Thrive Hair Bar, we believe in the transformative power of embracing one’s natural beauty, and we are dedicated to helping our clients feel confident, empowered and utterly fabulous in their natural glory.Internship ResponsibilitiesThis role expectations are that you dedicate at least 8 hours per week to Thrive Hair Bar assignments which are due 1x per week, attend weekly one on ones with the CEO, and work in person at least 1x per week for a minimum of 2 hours. Some assignments require in-person work and some assignments can be done remotely.The responsibilities include creating & editing marketing materials including emails, social media posts, website posts, videos, an internal newsletter, the marketing calendar and recruiting model talent.Internship BenefitsWork closely with a startup CEO from Columbia and UPENNsee behind the scenes of what launching a new company from the ground up is like$100 monthly stipend1 free hair appointment per monthand you are invited to all Thrive Hair Bar team activities!Submission GuidelinesPlease submit 4 items: 1. Cover Letter in PDF Format, 2. Writing Sample (this can be something you created in the past/for a class) in PDF OR JPG FORMAT, 3. Social Media Post/Video/ReelSample (something with imagery) YOU CAN LINK TO YOUR INSTAGRAM OR SEND A PDF OR JPG IMAGE/POST, 4. Resume in PDF format

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Internship - Teaching Artist - Music at TBEY Arts Center, Inc.
jobs.description: Employer: TBEY Arts Center, Inc. Expires: 06/26/2026 Internship - Teaching Artist - MusicDo You Love the arts? Would you like to teach music to children?Milwaukee, WI TBEY Arts Center, Inc.Teaching Artist Position (College Internship)Base Pay Rate: $25 per hour (based on experience)Position: Teaching Artist - MusicType: Part Time / Hourly / Seasonal (Must be available from Aug-Jun)About TBEY Arts Center, Inc.TBEY Arts Center strengthens youth and young adults through exposure to the arts and opportunities for creative expression. Youth and young adults ages 2-19 receive professional arts instruction, enjoy performances and exhibits, and receive tutoring, mentoring and coaching to achieve academically and pursue their interest in the arts.OverviewTBEY Arts Center, Inc. is seeking qualified candidates for the position of Teaching Artist. Teaching artist will provide art instruction to students at assigned school(s) for the 2024-2025 school year. Teaching Artists will work closely with program staff for curriculum support. Each Teaching Artist will be provided with professional development (artistically and classroom management). TBEY Arts Center, Inc. program staff will be the point of contact for the schoolKey ResponsibilitiesPlanning:Develop an arts curriculum, including weekly lesson plans, aligned with Wisconsin Arts Education Standards. TBEY program staff will support in tools and resources needed.Attending meetings with and collaborate with TBEY program and artistic staff as needed.Participate in ongoing professional development throughout the school year.Arts Instruction:Provide in-class arts instruction and engagement to students at assigned school(s) through active learning, dialogue, and hands-on experiences. Support will be provided by the school’s staff (paraprofessionals, classroom teachers, safety and other school support.)Maintain and organize classroom supplies to support lesson objectives and align with curriculum standards.Required QualificationsMinimum 1-2 years of experience working with youth and arts.Must be available between August – June.Passionate about the arts and sharing your skills.Knowledge and experience planning and developing arts lessons.Knowledge and experience in classroom management and instructional methods.Experience working with Google and Microsoft Teams programs.Demonstrate clear communication skills.How to applySubmit the following to Info@tbey.org:A cover letter detailing your interest in the role and relevant experience.A professional resume (artist resume in addition is highly recommended)Example lesson plans and/or artistic projects you have led/created.Two professional and/or artistic references that can attest to your ability to carry out the duties of this position.CompensationHourly Rate: $25 per hour (based on experience).Part time (10-35 hours per week)Application DeadlineYou can send application materials to info@tbey.orgEqual Opportunity EmploymentTBEY Arts Center, Inc. is an equal opportunity employer and encourages applications from all qualified individuals, regardless of background.Non-Discrimination PolicyTBEY Arts Center, Inc. is committed to providing a welcoming and inclusive environment for all individuals, regardless of race, color, ethnicity, national origin, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, or socioeconomic status. We believe that diversity is essential to achieving our mission of promoting access to arts and fostering creativity among youth. As such, we have a zero-tolerance policy for any form of discrimination, harassment, or bullying. We strive to create a safe and supportive environment where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to participate fully in our programs. Our commitment to non-discrimination extends to all aspects of our organization, including our programs, employment practices, and community engagements.By embracing diversity and promoting equity, TBEY Arts Center, Inc. aims to create a vibrant, creative community where all young people can explore their artistic potential and express themselves freely.This is a college Internship.Special Notes/InstructionsSubmit to info@tbey.org: A cover letter detailing your interest in the role and relevant experience. A professional resume Example lesson plans and/or artistic projects you have led/created. Two professional and/or artistic referencesContact Informationinfo@tbey.org 414.562.8239Apply Now

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Florence Price Archival Internship at University of Arkansas - University Libraries
jobs.description: Employer: University of Arkansas - University Libraries Expires: 06/28/2026 Special Collections is home to the papers of composer Florence Price, a Little Rock native who was the first Black woman to have one of her symphonic compositions performed by a major orchestra in the United States; her papers are regularly among Special Collections’ most-used collections, and symphonies and performers around the world have used her original manuscripts to inform new performances and new interpretations of her works. Drawing on secondary scholarly resources as well as archival collection materials and their own musical knowledge, the intern will propose metadata enhancements to improve the utility and accessibility of finding aids for five collections related Price, with a particular focus on the information needs of researchers intending to perform Price’s works. The intern will then work with their supervisor to update the collection finding aids, including gaining experience with ArchivesSpace, the content management system used by Special Collections. Depending on the length of the internship and the interests of the intern, they may also perform similar work on other collections, such as the papers of composers William Grant Still or Laurence Powell or the organizational records of Opera in the Ozarks, or they may propose another related project, such as creation of or updates to a Special Collections LibGuide. The intern will also create a public-facing product or event in order to share about the work they have done; the format will be determined by the intern in consultation with their supervisor, but might take the form of a blog post, a public presentation, a guided listening session, a pop-up exhibit, a musical performance, or some other activity or publication. The position will be for the fall 2026 semester or for both the fall 2026 and spring 2027 semesters, depending on the successful applicant’s desired term length. Minimum Qualifications: Current graduate student pursuing a Master of Music or a Certificate in Black Sacred Music at the University of Arkansas, FayettevilleExperience with Microsoft Word and Excel Preferred Qualifications: Previous experience with archival researchFamiliarity with the work of Florence Price Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities: Knowledge of music composition practicesStrong familiarity with a variety of forms of classical music, including orchestral compositions, vocal compositions, piano music, and chamber worksExcellent written communication skills

jobs.title: Graphic Designer Intern at Trilliant Food & Nutrition
jobs.description: Employer: Trilliant Food & Nutrition Expires: 06/28/2026 About Us:Trilliant Food & Nutrition is a leading manufacturer of premium coffees and powdered beverages, proudly headquartered in Little Chute, Wisconsin. With a legacy rooted in quality, innovation, and service, Trilliant delivers branded and private label solutions across retail, foodservice, and e-commerce channels. Horseshoe Beverage Company, based in Neenah, WI, is a leading ready-to-drink beverage manufacturer dedicated to delivering an exceptional beverage experience. By combining industry-leading talent and cutting-edge equipment, we drive innovation and consistently deliver the highest quality products to our customers—first and fast. Our brand portfolio includes Victor Allen’s®, Dutch Bros® beverages and Nurri™, our better-for-you protein beverage line. We are passionate about beverages – and about building a team that’s just as energized. We invite you to explore opportunities at Trilliant or Horseshoe, to see if your talents and career aspirations may fit with our openings. Position Overview:The graphic design intern will support the in-house creative team through a variety of brand expression projects such as digital & e-commerce, web, print, packaging, signage, mock-ups, sales collateral, brand identity, and more. Work closely with the Director of Creative on brand design/creative strategies and evolving internal creative processes that enable and create efficiencies within the organization. Develop and push ‘big ideas’ that enhance and grow creative output within Trilliant through ideation sessions, development and creative reviews. A great opportunity to learn more about the creative industry through a nationwide food and beverage organization while building out your portfolio. Responsibilities:Support the creative team on creative activation support, including but not limited to: digital ads, social graphics, website design, e-commerce brand page design, internal communication support.Communicate with external agency partners on creative needs, providing feedback and guiding creative decisions.Competitive brand design research. Support the Sr. Graphic designer on product mock-ups, renders, customer meeting collateral, site signage and in-store experience collateral.Research industry tools that enhance and evolve internal processes.Brand Identity development support for internal and external brands.Participate and/or organize creative reviews and walk-the-wall sessions. Qualifications:Student working toward a Bachelor’s degree or associate's degree in Graphic Design, User Experience Design or Multimedia Design. Junior or Senior level status. GPA: 3.0 or higher is preferred. Good communication skills with the ability to collaborate. Strong knowledge of Adobe Creative Cloud. Impressive portfolio, demonstrating recent work.Ability to build strong working relationships internally and externally. Hours, Location and Pay: We offer a competitive base pay rate and benefits package for full-time employees. This role works standard business hours Monday - Friday at our Little Chute, WI manufacturing facility. Physical and Mental Demands:While performing the duties of this job, the employee is frequently required to sit, hear, use hands to type data, use a personal computer and telephone. This employee may occasionally have to operate business machines and lift and/move up to 50 pounds.Specific vision abilities required in this job include close vision and the ability to adjust focus. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.Mental demands include multi-tasking, decision making, problem solving, comparing, copying, computing, compiling, analyzing, coordinating, and synthesizing data.Potential for exposure to chemicals, heights, loud noises, mechanical equipment, wet environments, and other potential hazards exists. Safety Statement:At Trilliant and Horseshoe, safety is every employee’s first responsibility. We expect all employees to adhere to all safety practices, have the moral courage to stop other individuals from performing unsafe acts, and immediately report unsafe conditions. Disclosures:The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by people assigned to this classification. They are not to be construed as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, and skills required of personnel so classified. All personnel may be required to perform duties outside of their normal responsibilities from time to time, as needed.Responsible for following food safety/regulatory policies and procedures, executing responsibilities as identified in standard operating procedures, and reporting food safety/regulatory concerns to the Production or Quality Manager.Trilliant Food & Nutrition and Horseshoe Beverage Company are Drug Free Workplaces. All applicants are subject to a drug screen and background check as a condition of employment.Trilliant Food and Nutrition and Horseshoe Beverage participate in the E-Verify process.EEO/AA including Vets and DisabledIf you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the employment process, please contact us by email at HR@trilliantfood.com and let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.Trilliant Food and Nutrition and Horseshoe Beverage are Equal Opportunity Employers.The principles of the OECD and UN have been taken into consideration and used as guidance in our human rights practices and procedures.

If so, our Lamar Corporate office in Baton Rouge, Louisiana is now hiring a new National Sales Intern. Lamar Advertising has also been named as a “Best Company to Work For” in U.S. News & World Report’s annual ranking.Learn more about us on our official YouTube channel.Check reviews and company updates on our Glassdoor page.Learn more about our Great Place to Work certification.What you can expect from us:A part-time schedule (10-15 hours a week) between 8:00 am - 5:00 pm, depending on your school scheduleAn hourly rate of $15 / hourA 2-3 week comprehensive training programWhat we're looking for in you:Strong organizational skills with attention to detailComfortable managing deadlines and shifting prioritiesProficient in Microsoft Office, especially Excel and WordFamiliarity with Adobe Suite (preferred, but not required)Excellent verbal and written communication skillsAbility to work independently and take initiativeComfortable using social media and online platforms for research or communication Education and Experience:High School diploma or equivalent requiredMust be in current pursuit of an Associate's or Bachelor’s degree in a related field of studyConcentration in Graphic Design, Advertising, or Marketing preferredPlease note: Upon submitting your application, you will be asked to complete an 8-15 minute personality assessment. Not completing this assessment could result in disqualification from consideration for this position.Candidates with a disability in need of an accommodation to fulfill our application requirements should email recruiting@lamar.com.A day in the life:Run and compile monthly performance and sales activity reportsComplete and submit expense reports on behalf of executives and team membersSupport the planning, coordination, and execution of employee engagement eventsAssist with logistics and preparation for client-facing events and functionsCommunicate with local markets and internal support teams to gather informationProvide Proof of Performance materials (e.g., advertisement photos, webcams)Organize and maintain digital files using Google Drive and HighspotAssist with the creation and collection of sales collateral and materialsPerform other administrative tasks and special projects as assigned Physical Demands and Work EnvironmentThe primary work environment is an office.The specific physical demands/requirements of the job include: lifting less than 25% of the time, reading, color distinction, acuity, sitting greater than 50% of the time, and talking.The typical percentage of time spent traveling and spending nights away from home is less than 10%.Who we are:Founded in 1902, Lamar Advertising Company is one of the largest outdoor advertising companies in the world. With over 351,000 displays across the United States and Canada, Lamar is dedicated to helping both local businesses and national brands reach broad audiences every day.We provide ad space through:BillboardsInterstate logosHandpainted muralsTransportation and airportsThe largest network of digital billboards in the United StatesWe live by the Golden Rule, and we operate with honesty and integrity in every aspect of our business. We are open with our employees, transparent with our customers, and loyal to the communities in which we serve. While Lamar is a large company, each office has its own culture and family atmosphere, making employees feel connected both locally and nationally.We are committed to sustainable and environmentally friendly business practices and use a number of innovative strategies to reduce our environmental impact. We are actively working to reduce our annual greenhouse gas emissions and are projecting a 70% decrease by 2026 as a result of our efforts.Lamar is an EEO/AA employer including Individuals with Disabilities, Protected Veterans, and any other State or Federally protected characteristic.Please note: Lamar Advertising does not sponsor or take over sponsorship of a work visa. Only candidates with U.S. work authorization not requiring sponsorship will be considered for employment.SMS and Email Communications: By providing your contact information and submitting this form, you agree to receive email and SMS communications from Lamar Advertising Company regarding job opportunities, hiring events, and career-related updates. Message and data rates may apply. You can opt out at any time by emailing recruiting@lamar.com or replying 'STOP' to text messages. Your information will be processed in accordance with our privacy policy.Disability Self-Identification: When applying for a job with Lamar, you will be asked to voluntarily self-identify whether you have a disability. Please take a moment to watch this video for clarification on why we're asking for this information!California Residents - Lamar collects personal information in the ordinary course of considering job applicants. This information may include, for example, name, address, phone number and other contact information, employment history and reference contact information, and any other information provided by an applicant to Lamar. By submitting an application, you consent to the use or sharing of this personal information solely for the purpose of consideration for employment by Lamar. Lamar will not sell this information.#CorpID #EarlyTalent

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Worship Intern at Front Range Church
jobs.description: Employer: Front Range Church Expires: 06/28/2026 Job TitleWorship Intern (Paid) – Worship Ministry & LeadershipPosition Overview: Worship InternshipMany people feel called into worship ministry but haven’t yet had the opportunity to experience what that looks like in a real church environment. This Worship Internship is designed to provide hands-on experience, training, coaching, and development to help you explore and grow in your calling as a worship leader.As a Worship Intern, you will serve within a modern worship ministry, working closely with a Worship Pastor or Worship Leader. You will gain practical experience in leading worship, supporting services, and helping create meaningful, engaging environments where people can encounter God.This role is designed to develop both your musical abilities and your leadership capacity while giving you exposure to the realities of church ministry.Training & DevelopmentYou will receive coaching and structured development tailored specifically to worship ministry. This includes hands-on training in:Supporting and occasionally leading worship in weekend services and midweek environmentsInstrument/Vocal development and stage presenceLearning basic musical arrangement and band dynamicsUnderstanding service flow (set building, transitions, dynamics)Collaborating with production teams and other ministry areasIn addition to musical training, you will:Learn from experienced worship pastors and leadersParticipate in leadership development and spiritual growth opportunitiesGrow in areas such as communication, teamwork, and reliabilityHave opportunities to observe and gradually take on more responsibility in servicesYou will be given opportunities to contribute in real environments, helping you build confidence, competence, and character over time.Length of InternshipThis internship is designed to provide meaningful, hands-on ministry experience over a defined season.Interns will typically serve for 9 months, with the potential for extension based on fit and availability.CompensationThis is a paid internship that includes a small, monthly stipend. Typical Weekly CommitmentWorship Intern: 20+ hours per weekSchedule will include weekends, rehearsals, and occasional special events.What You’ll GainReal-world experience in a modern worship ministryDevelopment as both a musician and emerging leaderHands-on experience serving on and supporting a volunteer teamMentorship and coaching from experienced worship leadersA clearer understanding of your calling and next steps in ministryPreferred QualificationsInterest in worship ministry and church leadershipBasic vocal or instrumental abilityWillingness to learn, receive feedback, and serve on a teamPositive attitude, reliability, and strong work ethicHow to ApplyFor more information or to apply, please email:worship@frontrange.org

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Social Media Marketing Intern at Nines Group
jobs.description: Employer: Nines Group Expires: 06/28/2026 Company OverviewNines Media is a social media marketing firm specializing in brand focused digital campaigns across major platforms. We work closely with restaurant franchises, beauty and skincare brands, lifestyle products, and consumer focused businesses to build long term brand presence through strategic content, short form video, and experiential marketing.Many of the brands and campaigns we support are influenced by Korean culture, including Korean food, beauty, entertainment, and lifestyle trends, while also collaborating with a wide range of diverse brands across multiple industries.In addition to managing digital campaigns, Nines Media directly operates retail locations, pop up events, and brand activations. This structure allows our marketing team to participate in both online content creation and in person brand execution.Position OverviewNines Media is seeking a Social Media Marketing Intern to support ongoing social media campaigns and content initiatives across multiple active brands. This role is primarily focused on social media execution, including content planning, filming support, editing, posting, and account management.This paid internship is best suited for recent graduates and early career professionals seeking hands on experience in a professional marketing environment. The position is structured as a long term opportunity, with full time employment frequently offered to high performing interns who demonstrate consistency, growth, and strong alignment with company goals.Primary Responsibilities- Support the creation and execution of social media content for active brand accounts- Assist with content planning, filming days, and campaign preparation- Edit short form video and visual assets for social media platforms- Support posting, scheduling, and general account maintenance- Engage with online audiences to support brand presence and community growth- Collaborate with internal teams during monthly campaigns and themed initiativesAdditional Experience Opportunities- Participation in retail events, pop up activations, and experiential marketing projects- On camera participation for content creation is recommended but not required- Occasional domestic and international travel opportunities, offered based on productivity, performance, and project needs- Professional networking with brand partners, creators, and industry professionalsCandidate Qualifications- Strong interest in social media marketing and digital branding- Familiarity with major social media platforms and current content trends- Professional communication skills and attention to detail- Ability to work in a fast paced, collaborative environment- Reliability, initiative, and willingness to learnExperience with video editing, creative software, or marketing tools is preferred but not required.Applicants with a strong interest in Korean culture, Korean food, Korean beauty, Korean entertainment, and Korean media trends, as well as those who enjoy working with diverse brands, are likely to find this role especially engaging.Growth and Long Term OpportunityThis internship is designed as a pathway into long term employment. Nines Media prioritizes developing team members who seek stability and professional growth rather than short term internship experience. Interns who consistently perform at a high level are frequently offered extended roles or full time positions within the company.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Bilingual Production & Brand Intern (Global Operations) at Magic Wave
jobs.description: Employer: Magic Wave Expires: 06/28/2026 We aren't looking for a "coffee-runner." We are looking for a high-potential Bilingual Intern to act as a force multiplier for our leadership team. You will directly support our Production Operations Lead and Brand Strategy Lead in executing international audio projects.If you live at the intersection of Fan Economy, Celebrity Culture, and Business Operations, this is your playground. You will get hands-on experience in how a global startup turns narrative content into a cult-following brand.Key ResponsibilitiesOperational Support: Assist the Production Operations Lead with project tracking, script critique (English), and coordinating with our global network of voice talent.Brand & Research: Support the Brand Strategy Lead in conducting deep-dive research on fan behavior, celebrity trends, and emerging social media platforms.Bilingual Bridge: Facilitate seamless communication between our US and Asia teams, ensuring that creative nuances are preserved across languages.Content Assistance: Help create engaging social media assets and assist in the basic editing of audio/video content for our marketing channels.Pipeline Health: Maintain high-quality documentation and asset management within our production pipeline.Might need support for YouTube channel operationWhat We’re Looking ForAcademic Background: Current student or recent grad majoring in Media, Film, Communication, or Business.Bilingual Mastery: Professional-level fluency in English and Chinese is non-negotiable. You must be able to translate not just words, but cultural contexts.Pop Culture Junkie: A genuine obsession with Fan Economy and Celebrity Trends. You should understand why certain stars "break the internet" and how fans mobilize.Technical Literacy: Familiarity with Lark/Office suit, CapCut, or Canva.The "Startup Soul": You are proactive, "fierce" in execution, and don't wait to be told what to do next. You thrive in fast-paced, evolving environments.Why This Internship MattersReal Leverage: You aren't just observing; you are providing the tactical support that allows our senior leads to scale.Global Ecosystem: Exposure to a unique production model that bridges Hollywood aesthetics with Asian market speed.Mentorship: Direct access to a team of award-winning producers and strategic partners.The Full-Time Track: We hire for keeps. Exceptional interns are prioritized for the “Lead-Track” career path within the company.How to ApplySend your Resume and a Brief Cover Letter (tell us why you are a good fit and/or showcase the project/film that best reflects your creative talent) to: hr@metaelementsinc.com

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: Social Media and Content Intern at Cato Institute
jobs.description: Employer: Cato Institute Expires: 06/29/2026 To be considered for this role, you must apply directly through our online application. OverviewCato's Social Media team oversees the institute's presence on platforms with over 1.7 million followers, including Facebook, LinkedIn, X/Twitter, Instagram, and TikTok — turning the work of Cato's scholars and researchers into content that reaches and engages audiences interested in individual liberty, limited government, free markets, and peace. The team handles the entire content creation process: finding newsworthy angles in Cato's research, developing platform-specific posts, graphics, and short videos, managing publishing schedules, and analyzing performance data to shape strategy. Interns work across all platforms and participate in regular cross-team meetings to stay updated on active scholarship and identify opportunities to transform Cato research into timely, audience-appropriate content. The environment is fast-paced and news-driven — strong candidates are self-motivated, platform-savvy, and comfortable producing content independently under tight deadlines.ResponsibilitiesCreate platform-native social media content across Facebook, LinkedIn, X/Twitter, Instagram, and TikTok — including posts, threads, graphics, and short-form videos — translating Cato research and commentary for broad audience.Create short-form videos and Reels for Instagram and TikTok, including scripting, editing, and captioning with CapCut, Adobe Premiere, or similar tools.Create graphics and visual assets for social media using Canva, Adobe, or similar tools.Attend regular meetings with teams across Cato to identify timely research and commentary worth sharing on social media.Manage and uphold publishing calendars; plan and release content across platforms.Track trends, news cycles, and platform algorithm updates to identify timely content opportunities and communicate findings with the team.Monitor platform analytics and KPIs with native tools; support performance reports and strategic insights.Assist with digital advertising efforts and email marketing campaigns as assigned.Required QualificationsStrong interest in sharing public policy research with broad audiences via social media; shows alignment with Cato's principles of individual liberty, limited government, free markets, and peace.Proven experience managing social media accounts or content calendars — through a campus organization, think tank, advocacy group, media outlet, or independent content project — with measurable results.Effective social media writing — clear, concise, and tailored to platform standards and audience expectations across channels.Comfort with native platform analytics and KPIs; ability to interpret performance data and turn it into content decisions.Experience creating short-form video content for Instagram Reels, TikTok, or similar platforms, including basic editing and captioning.Experience creating social media graphics with Canva, Adobe, or similar tools.Self-motivated, organized, and reliable — capable of working independently, meeting deadlines in a fast-paced news environment, and managing multiple content streams at once.Professionalism, reliability, and attention to detail — social media work demands precision in written content, brand consistency, and reliable follow-through across platforms and deadlines.Preferred QualificationsExperience with video editing software such as CapCut, Adobe Premiere, or similar tools for short-form social media content.Experience with social media management and scheduling tools.Experience managing digital advertising campaigns on social media platforms.Prior experience in a communications, media, or marketing environment demonstrating audience growth or engagement.A content portfolio or active social media presence demonstrating platform fluency and creative range; finalists may be asked to submit a portfolio showcasing social media and content creation experience.The Cato Internship ProgramCato’s paid internships are available for undergraduates, recent graduates, graduate students, law students, and early-career professionals who are strongly committed to individual liberty, limited government, free markets, and peace—principles that together form libertarianism, also known as “classical liberalism,” “market liberalism,” or, to many of our international friends, simply “liberalism.” All Cato interns participate in the same intensive seminar series, which covers a wide range of history, philosophy, policy, and professional development topics. Interns also assist with events and occasionally support Cato staff with other daily tasks. Interns receive competitive pay. Part-time roles are adjusted accordingly and require a minimum of 25 hours per week. Program participants must be able to attend in person in Washington, DC.For more information about the internship program and experience, we encourage you to explore our website. If you have any questions, email studentprograms@cato.org.

jobs.description: Employer: Cato Institute Expires: 06/29/2026 To be considered for this role, you must apply directly through our online application. OverviewLibertarianism.org is Cato's dedicated platform for the theory, history, and ideas of liberty — publishing long-form articles, intellectual history essays, podcast episodes, and social media content for a broad audience interested in the foundations of a free society. The site's content spans classical liberal philosophy, the American founding, economics, and contemporary policy, translated for general audiences who value individual liberty.Interns mainly support the marketing and content operations team by building and maintaining analytics databases, producing quarterly performance reports, creating video and social media content, and tracking KPIs across platforms. The work is ideal for applicants with a background in marketing, communications, or content creation who also have a genuine intellectual interest in libertarian ideas and can engage seriously with the material they're promoting.ResponsibilitiesBuild and maintain a marketing analytics database that tracks KPIs across social media, the website, newsletter, and podcast platforms.Produce quarterly marketing analytics reports, synthesizing performance data across channels into clear insights and strategic recommendations for the team.Track trending topics and viral content related to libertarian ideas and the wider political and intellectual scene; share insights with the team.Research libertarian perspectives on policy and intellectual history topics; summarize and adapt content for Libertarianism.org's audience.Create social media posts and graphics using Canva or similar tools; edit short-form video content from archival and original footage with Adobe Premiere or comparable software.Review podcast episodes, extract quotes and context for marketing and promotion.Collect quotes from libertarian writers and thinkers to share on social media platforms.Support other content and communication projects as assignedRequired QualificationsGenuine interest in the libertarian intellectual tradition and demonstrated alignment with Cato's principles of individual liberty, limited government, free markets, and peace — applicants should be comfortable engaging seriously with the ideas they are helping promote.Background in marketing, communications, or a related field; able to quickly read, grasp, and accurately summarize complex texts for a general audienceProficiency in Microsoft Excel for building and maintaining analytics databases and producing performance reportsFamiliar with social media platforms and comfortable tracking analytics across channels, including X/Twitter, Instagram, and YouTube.Experience designing content with Canva or similar tools.Professionalism, reliability, and attention to detail — this work involves managing multiple content streams and meeting regular reporting deadlines.Preferred QualificationsExperience using video editing software like Adobe Premiere, CapCut, or similar tools for short-form social media content.Knowledge of social media analytics platforms or marketing reporting tools.Prior experience in content creation, marketing, or communications roles — including campus media, think tanks, advocacy organizations, or independent content projects.Applicants are encouraged to review Libertarianism.org's recent publications and the Portraits of Liberty podcast prior to applying.The Cato Internship ProgramCato’s paid internships are available for undergraduates, recent graduates, graduate students, law students, and early-career professionals who are strongly committed to individual liberty, limited government, free markets, and peace—principles that together form libertarianism, also known as “classical liberalism,” “market liberalism,” or, to many of our international friends, simply “liberalism.” All Cato interns participate in the same intensive seminar series, which covers a wide range of history, philosophy, policy, and professional development topics. Interns also assist with events and occasionally support Cato staff with other daily tasks. Interns receive competitive pay. Part-time roles are adjusted accordingly and require a minimum of 25 hours per week. Program participants must be able to attend in person in Washington, DC.For more information about the internship program and experience, we encourage you to explore our website. If you have any questions, email studentprograms@cato.org.

jobs.feedName: Mike Curb College of Arts, Media, & Communication
jobs.title: World Cup Fan Growth Intern - LA at Glitchcore Labs Inc.
jobs.description: Employer: Glitchcore Labs Inc. Expires: 06/29/2026 World Cup Growth Intern — Los AngelesAbout UsWe are a fast-growing AI video startup building the next generation of social co-creation platforms. Our product turns everyday videos into fun, interactive, AI-powered experiences that people can create, remix, share, and play with.We are launching a Los Angeles growth campaign around the 2026 soccer season, with a special focus on Team USA fans, soccer culture, match-day energy, and the fan communities gathering around SoFi Stadium and across LA.This is not a traditional campus ambassador role. It is a hybrid growth role combining online user growth, social media operations, creator outreach, community building, and offline fan activation.We are looking for someone young, creative, high-energy, and extremely good with people — someone who understands social media, knows how to create hype, can talk to strangers, can DM creators, can organize small activations, and can turn fan excitement into real user growth. The RoleAs a World Cup Fan Growth Intern, your mission is to help Wapoo become part of the soccer fan experience in Los Angeles — both online and offline.You will help us reach Team USA fans, soccer communities, young creators, students, local fan groups, sports bars, watch parties, and high-energy public spaces around LA. You will work on both digital growth and real-world activation: finding users online, bringing them into the product, creating social media buzz, and then turning that buzz into offline participation around major soccer moments.This role is ideal for someone who is creative, social, fast-moving, and comfortable working in ambiguity. You should be able to come up with ideas, test them quickly, talk to people, post content, reach out to communities, and help us understand what actually drives young users to try and share a new social app.You will not just be “handing out flyers.” You will help us design growth experiments, run online campaigns, recruit early users, organize local fan activations, build lightweight partnerships, collect feedback, and create viral moments around Team USA and the 2026 soccer season. Key ResponsibilitiesOnline User Growth & Social Media OperationsHelp operate and grow Wapoo’s local LA social channels across TikTok, Instagram, and other relevant platforms.Create, test, and optimize short-form content ideas around Team USA, soccer fans, World Cup moments, fan rivalry, AI video effects, and LA youth culture.Identify online communities where soccer fans and young users gather, including Instagram pages, TikTok creators, Discord groups, Reddit communities, student groups, sports communities, and local event pages.Help turn online attention into real user actions: app downloads, sign-ups, content creation, shares, event participation, and referrals.Track what types of posts, hooks, captions, comments, DMs, and community messages actually convert users. Creator, Community & Partnership OutreachReach out to local creators, student influencers, soccer creators, sports meme pages, fan accounts, event organizers, photographers, videographers, and community leaders.Build lightweight collaborations with people or groups who can help Wapoo reach Team USA fans and young LA users.Help recruit early users from LA colleges, soccer communities, creator circles, nightlife groups, sports bars, and fan events.Write and test outreach messages for Instagram DM, email, Discord, Reddit, group chats, and other community channels.Maintain relationships with high-potential creators, organizers, and early users. Offline Fan Activation & Field MarketingPlan and execute offline promotions around SoFi Stadium, Team USA fan gatherings, soccer watch parties, sports bars, public fan zones, college areas, and high-traffic LA locations.Create fun, memorable, and shareable fan moments that make people want to scan, try Wapoo, post, and invite friends.Test street-team tactics including stickers, posters, QR codes, mini challenges, fan contests, photo/video moments, giveaways, and surprise activations.Approach people in person, explain Wapoo quickly, and get them excited enough to try the product on the spot.Collect live feedback from users and report what messaging, locations, hooks, and activations worked best. Creative Growth ExperimentsBrainstorm and execute creative ways to make Wapoo part of the soccer fan experience — not just another app promotion.Design small, fast experiments around Team USA support, fan identity, match-day predictions, rivalry, AI video transformations, and social sharing.Help develop online-to-offline growth loops, such as TikTok challenges that lead to fan meetups, QR-code activations that lead to social sharing, or creator videos that drive event attendance.Work with the team to turn offline activations into online content for TikTok, Instagram, and other short-form platforms.Think creatively about how to make Wapoo feel fun, viral, and culturally relevant during the 2026 soccer season. Local Operations & ExecutionHelp coordinate field materials, stickers, posters, QR codes, small giveaways, content shoots, event logistics, and user recruitment workflows.Move fast, communicate clearly, and take ownership of execution in a startup environment.Be comfortable working around busy public spaces, outdoor events, fan crowds, sports bars, campuses, and creator/community events.Report progress clearly, including outreach numbers, user feedback, activation results, content performance, and growth learnings. Schedule: Part-time to full-time availability preferred during major activation weeks. Minimum RequirementsCurrently based in Los Angeles or able to work in person around the LA area.Strong interest in soccer, sports culture, fan communities, social media, live events, or youth culture.Experience or strong interest in at least one of the following: online user growth, social media operations, creator outreach, community building, field marketing, event marketing, or campus/community promotion.Comfortable reaching out to people online through DMs, email, group chats, Discord, Reddit, or other community channels.Outgoing personality with strong people skills. You should be comfortable talking to strangers, pitching ideas, and getting people excited.Creative mindset. We want someone who can come up with fun ideas, not just follow instructions.Familiar with TikTok, Instagram, memes, short-form video culture, and how young people discover and share new apps.Strong execution ability. You can organize small campaigns, run local promotions, and solve problems on the fly.Available to work flexible hours, especially evenings, weekends, fan events, creator events, and major match days.Able to work independently in a fast-moving startup environment. Preferred QualificationsExperience in social media operations, online growth, creator marketing, field marketing, street team promotion, event planning, sports marketing, nightlife promotion, or community building.Experience promoting apps, consumer products, parties, clubs, concerts, sports events, student events, or creator campaigns.Existing connections with LA soccer communities, Team USA fan groups, sports bars, student groups, creators, local event organizers, or youth communities.Strong “cold outreach” ability — you know how to write DMs, start conversations, follow up, and turn strangers into collaborators or users.Strong social confidence and hustler mentality. You know how to get attention without being awkward or pushy.Good taste in viral ideas, memes, short-form content, fan culture, and Gen Z internet language.Students or recent graduates from LA-area schools are welcome, including USC, UCLA, LMU, CSUN, CSULA, Santa Monica College, Long Beach State, UCI, Chapman, Cal State Fullerton, SDSU, UCSD, and other nearby schools.

We are looking for active, tech-savvy college students to join us as Remote Campus Brand Ambassadors. In this role, you will bypass the traditional corporate red tape and directly drive organic community growth and digital PR for globally renowned electronics flagships, including Anker and TCL.Why This is the Ultimate Student Side-Gig (Your Pain Points Solved):🎓 Zero Class Conflicts: 100% asynchronous. No mandatory Zoom meetings, no rigid 9-to-5 clock-ins. Work around your exam schedules and lectures.📝 Resume Goldmine: Stop putting "unrelated retail work" on your CV. Add official bullets like Organic Growth Marketing, Cross-Border Digital PR, and Community Asset Management to impress future Fortune 500 employers.💰 Uncapped Financial Freedom: Earn far beyond standard student hourly wages. We offer a tiered compensation structure that rewards your efficiency and content traction.Core Responsibilities:Community Seeding: Translate premium brand selling points into native internet slang, memes, or informative threads, and deploy them across mainstream sub-communities.Organic Engagement: Foster natural word-of-mouth discussions, managing the comment sections to naturally capture consumer attention.Compliance Tracking: Study and navigate platform guidelines to optimize post longevity and visibility.Position Requirements (Who You Are):Active Social Media Native: You must have active, established personal profiles on Reddit and/or X (Twitter).
